_meta.description: Curated dataset of Cyberpunk 2077 shards, extracted from wiki category pages. Entity extraction performed manually from shard titles and known lore.

shards.title: Archived Conversation: Brick and Ellis

location.linked_gig: Cyberpsycho Sighting: Where the Bodies Hit the Floor

shards.summary: Ellis warns Brick about Royce plotting to seize leadership of Maelstrom. Brick urges Ellis to flee.

shards.title: Archived Conversation: Bobby and Frank

location.linked_gig: Cyberpsycho Sighting: Phantom of Night City

shards.summary: Frank notices Bobby's increasing cyberpsychosis symptoms. Bobby denies it.

shards.title: Archived Conversation: Cedric Muller and Maciej Nakonieczny

location.linked_gig: Cyberpsycho Sighting: Smoke on the Water

shards.summary: Two Arasaka ex-employees discuss being discarded by the corporation and their descent.

shards.title: Archived Conversation: Dr. Martin Sypura and Lt. Mower
shards.summary: A police lieutenant tries to get psychiatric clearance from a doctor for a soldier showing psychosis signs.

shards.title: Archived Conversation: Fred Reid and Mor Jones

location.linked_gig: Cyberpsycho Sighting: Where the Bodies Hit the Floor

shards.summary: Fred confesses to Mor his growing numbness and disconnection from his own body.

shards.title: Archived Conversation: Hideyoshi Ueno and Ken Masuda

location.linked_gig: Cyberpsycho Sighting: Ticket to the Major Leagues

shards.summary: A rising Arasaka executive pressures a colleague while showing signs of dehumanization.

shards.title: Archived Conversation: Hoof and Sanders

location.linked_gig: Cyberpsycho Sighting: Where the Bodies Hit the Floor

shards.summary: Two former soldiers discuss the toll of combat cyberware on their mental state.

shards.title: Archived Conversation: John and Deng

location.linked_gig: Cyberpsycho Sighting: House on a Hill

shards.summary: Deng is worried about John's increasingly erratic behaviour after heavy cyberware modifications.

shards.title: Archived Conversation: Julia Lee and Norio Akuhara
shards.summary: A Militech-contracted therapist attempts to assess Norio's psychological state post-augmentation.

shards.title: Archived Conversation: Ken Masuda and Diego Ramirez

location.linked_gig: Cyberpsycho Sighting: Ticket to the Major Leagues

shards.summary: Ken Masuda pressures Diego about performance metrics, showing signs of extreme corp-induced psychosis.

shards.title: Archived Conversation: Maciej Nakonieczny and Peter Novotny

location.linked_gig: Cyberpsycho Sighting: Smoke on the Water

shards.summary: Peter tries to convince Maciej to seek help, noting his mental deterioration.

shards.title: "Saigon Sisters" - TREATMENT

location.linked_gig: Cyberpsycho Sighting: Phantom of Night City

shards.summary: A medical treatment file for a pair of cyberpsychos who operated as a duo.

shards.title: Archived Conversation: Lamarr and Dr. Koch
shards.summary: Dr. Koch is documenting Lamarr's accelerating dissociation from reality.

shards.title: Archived Conversation: Lucas McClintock and Jimmy Fallen
shards.summary: Jimmy confesses to Lucas his addiction to combat stims and fear of losing control.

shards.title: Archived Conversation: Nasim Perlman and Tucker Albach
shards.summary: Nasim and Tucker, two Militech operators, debate whether Tucker's behaviour is still within norms.

shards.title: Archived Conversation: Nathan Goodwin and Boris Skorsky
shards.summary: Boris is deeply troubled by Nathan's new philosophy that flesh is an obstacle to transcendence.

shards.title: Archived Conversation: Pvt. Jacquelyn Dennis and Senior NCO Cat Menendez
shards.summary: A superior officer files a report on a soldier showing psychosis symptoms after combat implant surgery.

shards.title: Archived Conversation: Richard Ribisi and Pamela Ling
shards.summary: A fan's obsession with a pop star tips into dangerous territory in this chilling exchange.

shards.title: Archived Conversation: Sgt. Vincent Montez and Capt. Tomas Schonfeld
shards.summary: A captain files an urgent report on a sergeant who has gone AWOL after a combat stress breakdown.

shards.title: Archived Conversation: V. Trachev and Charles Bucks
shards.summary: Charles tries to convince Trachev to step away from his extreme body modification binge.

shards.title: Archived Conversation: Raheel Frayre and Julio Kingi
shards.summary: Julio tries to reach out to Raheel who has been isolated and increasingly violent since leaving the gang.

shards.title: Archived Conversation: Raudha Awang and Craig Giorgio
shards.summary: Craig is alarmed by Raudha's talk of feeling like a ghost in her own body after recent netrunner mods.

shards.title: Archived Conversation: Dr. Jack Allen and Dr. Tim Boone
shards.summary: Two doctors debate the ethics of prescribing combat cyberware knowing the psychosis risk.

shards.title: Archived Conversation: Dr. Joanne Koch and Dr. Fiona Vargas
shards.summary: Two trauma team doctors share clinical notes on a patient exhibiting advanced cyberpsychosis.

shards.title: Archived Conversation: Jorge Ron and Magdaline Juszczyk
shards.summary: Magdaline pleads with Jorge to report his partner's erratic behavior before someone gets hurt.

shards.title: Archived Conversation: Jordan M. and Dustin
shards.summary: Two nomads discuss the psychological toll of living on the fringe with heavy combat chrome.

shards.title: Archived Conversation: Larissa Almeida and Joe Peck
shards.summary: Joe is at a breaking point, and Larissa realises too late that her warnings went unheard.

shards.title: Archived Conversation: Larry Carel and Eve Baranski
shards.summary: Eve documents Larry's growing hostility and requests emergency psychiatric intervention.

shards.title: Archived Conversation: Laszlo Nagy and Elena "Pumpkin" Esposito
shards.summary: Elena tries to help Laszlo whose grip on reality is slipping after a botched ripperdoc job.

shards.title: Archived Conversation: Laura Decker and Martin McGrath
shards.summary: Laura tells Martin she is done with their relationship as he no longer feels human to her.

shards.title: Archived Conversation: Lauren Murphy and Artem Melnyk
shards.summary: Artem desperately asks Lauren for help after his combat subroutines start triggering without command.

shards.title: Archived Conversation: Lt. Suzume Hata and Haru Asai
shards.summary: An Arasaka lieutenant reports to internal security about a soldier who is becoming a liability.

shards.title: Archived Conversation: M. Araki and Satoko Ito
shards.summary: Satoko urges Araki to seek help before Arasaka notices and terminates his contract.

shards.title: Archived Conversation: Nadezhda Tiurina and Dr.
shards.summary: A clinical session where Nadezhda denies symptoms while the doctor's notes indicate otherwise.

shards.title: Archived Conversation: Nasim Perlman and Aldo Schroder
shards.summary: Aldo warns Nasim that the latest batch of military chrome has unacceptable side effects.

shards.title: Archived Conversation: Natalie Brooks and Matt Maksymowiz
shards.summary: Natalie notices Matt has stopped responding to her messages and reaches out in growing panic.

shards.title: Archived Conversation: Nathan Jacobs and Grace Jacobs
shards.summary: Grace, Nathan's sister, begs him to come home and stop the modifications before it is too late.

shards.title: Archived Conversation: Rex Ridgway and Arpana Bora
shards.summary: Rex tells Arpana about the voice in his head that feels more real than his own conscience.

shards.title: Archived Conversation: Reginald Keyes and J.T. Codd
shards.summary: Two low-level fixers discuss the rising number of chrome-crazed mercs ruining the market.

shards.title: The MaxTac Way, by Mathias Maddox
shards.summary: A first-person account of life in MaxTac, Night City's elite cyberpsycho response unit.

shards.title: America After the War
shards.summary: A historical overview of America's geopolitical state after the Fourth Corporate War.

shards.title: Arasaka Academy: Best of the Best, or Best for the Best?
shards.summary: A critical look at Arasaka's elite training academy and the question of meritocracy vs nepotism.

shards.title: Big 5 Employers in Night City – 2077
shards.summary: A business magazine rundown of the five dominant corporations employing Night City residents.

shards.title: Excerpts from "A History of the Nomads" by B.B. Pires
shards.summary: Scholarly excerpts examining nomad clan culture, history, and their rejection of corporate society.

shards.title: In the Shadows of Megabuildings
shards.summary: A journalistic piece on the living conditions of megabuilding residents and corporate neglect.

shards.title: Inside MetaCorp
shards.summary: Investigative piece on MetaCorp, a lesser-known subsidiary operating in the shadows of the Big Five.

shards.title: Lat-Am Wars
shards.summary: A retrospective on the corporate-funded conflicts in Latin America that shaped the modern world.

shards.title: Contemporary Netrunner Groups
shards.summary: A detailed look at the main netrunner factions active in 2077 and their competing agendas.

shards.title: Dogtown – Analytical Summary
shards.summary: An intelligence agency's strategic analysis of Dogtown's political situation and Barghest's control.

shards.title: Accident or sabotage?
shards.summary: An investigation into a suspicious industrial explosion, with corporate fingerprints everywhere.

shards.title: 75 Years of Cyberware, by Tsutomu Takahashi
shards.summary: A retrospective on the history of cyberware from early military applications to mass consumer adoption.

shards.title: Blaming Bartmoss - by 8ug8ear
shards.summary: A contrarian take on Rache Bartmoss's legacy, arguing his DataKrash harmed netrunners more than corps.

shards.title: Can cyberpsychosis be cured?
shards.summary: A medical journal article debating the treatability of cyberpsychosis versus NCPD's shoot-on-sight approach.

shards.title: Cyberware Usage and Its Side Effects
shards.summary: A corporate-issued document listing official side effects of cyberware — heavily sanitised.

shards.title: Binary Sigs vs Conspiracy Nuts
shards.summary: An online debate between a netrunner collective and conspiracy theorists about the Net's architecture.

shards.title: Beyond the Border of Empathy
shards.summary: A psychological study on empathy loss in heavily augmented individuals.

shards.title: Militech Datashard
shards.summary: An encrypted Militech datashard containing financial transaction records, obtained from Meredith Stout.

shards.title: Diary (117-121/77)
shards.summary: The private diary of Saburo Arasaka, revealing his fears about Yorinobu's rebellion and legacy planning.

shards.title: Operation Carpe Noctem
shards.summary: Classified operational briefing for an Arasaka night-time infiltration mission.

shards.title: PROJECT CONDIN SUSPENDED
shards.summary: An internal memo suspending a classified military development project with redacted reasons.

shards.title: Company Expense Account, Hasan Demir
shards.summary: Encrypted financial records of an Arasaka executive showing suspicious off-book expenditures.

shards.title: Destroy After Reading
shards.summary: A burn-on-read message involving a sensitive Militech operation. Contents barely decipherable.

shards.title: Draft Report - Dock Distribution
shards.summary: An encrypted draft report on logistics operations at Watson docks, flagging irregularities.

shards.title: Spellbook
shards.summary: An encrypted netrunner spellbook — collection of intrusion protocols from the Voodoo Boys.

shards.title: Sayonara Station, by Luke Steelman
shards.summary: A melancholic sci-fi short story about a man waiting at a space station for someone who will never come.

shards.title: Fanfiction: Johnny and the Shocking Truth
shards.summary: Fan-written fiction casting Johnny Silverhand in an absurd comedic adventure.

shards.title: Kibble and Scop
shards.summary: A bleak short story about food poverty and survival in Night City's lowest economic strata.

shards.title: Dr. Havoc & Radon
shards.summary: A pulp comic-book style story featuring Night City's most eccentric fictional duo.

shards.title: 1,000 Beats a Second
shards.summary: A short story told from the perspective of a concertgoer at a Samurai show in the 2020s.

shards.title: Chrome Night Love
shards.summary: A romantic short story set in Night City's underground about a merc and a ripperdoc.

shards.title: Fanfiction: An Omelet of Passion
shards.summary: An intentionally absurd piece of Johnny Silverhand fan-written romance fiction.

shards.title: "Watson Whore" Diary
shards.summary: The raw personal diary of a Watson resident working in the night economy, filled with dark humour.

shards.title: "Chrome and You" - Excerpt from a novel by Anonymous
shards.summary: A literary excerpt exploring the psychological relationship between a person and their cyberware.

shards.title: In the Shade of an Apple Tree, by Virginia Granchester
shards.summary: A pastoral nomad story about a child growing up outside Night City's reach.

shards.title: Corporate Wars: The Musical (shard)
shards.summary: A satirical musical script lampooning the corporate wars through show-tune numbers.

shards.title: WYSIWYG: What You Slot Is What You Got
shards.summary: A darkly comedic story about a man who gets terrible braindance software installed by a shady ripperdoc.

shards.title: "Samurai" Thirty Years Later: Facts, Myths, and More Myths
shards.summary: A retrospective music journalism piece on Samurai's legacy and the myths around the band's breakup.

shards.title: Big 5 Employers in Night City – 2077
shards.summary: Profiles of Night City's five biggest private sector employers and their key divisions.

shards.title: A Family of Empire
shards.summary: A profile of the Arasaka family dynasty, framed as a business journalism piece.

shards.title: Arasaka and Ornithology: Three Japanese Words to Know
shards.summary: A cultural piece explaining the Japanese ornithological symbols central to Arasaka's corporate identity.

shards.title: My grandpa was the King!
shards.summary: A tabloid-style piece about a Night City resident who claims royal lineage and the media circus around it.

shards.title: B.A. Guseinov, "Respiration and Intercellular Spaces."
shards.summary: A dry academic paper on bioengineering and its applications to cellular regeneration.

shards.title: Biased guide on Dogtown - Night City's Wild West
shards.summary: A deliberately one-sided tourist guide to Dogtown, ironically highlighting its lawlessness.

shards.title: New Musical Front Making Waves
shards.summary: A music press article covering the current underground music scene's reaction to Samurai's legacy.

shards.title: Archived Conversation
shards.summary: Marlon Fuscino writes to a wealthy hit-and-run driver announcing he has hired a killer to avenge his daughter.

shards.title: The Unofficial Diary of Lizzy Wizzy
shards.summary: Leaked diary entries from pop star Lizzy Wizzy, revealing the trauma behind her full chrome body conversion.

shards.title: Access Point Operation Protocol – Izanagi
shards.summary: An Arasaka technical document detailing protocols for accessing the Izanagi network nodes.

shards.title: Pain Editors and Mortality Rates
shards.summary: A medical study correlating pain editor cyberware usage with increased combat mortality rates.

shards.title: Make it Go Boom!
shards.summary: An enthusiast's guide to explosive cyberware modifications, tongue-in-cheek but technically detailed.

shards.title: Nomad cookbook from 2034
shards.summary: A practical survival cookbook from the Badlands nomad era, with recipes using scavenged materials.

shards.title: Chemicals: The Invisible Killer
shards.summary: A health advocacy article linking Petrochem's industrial operations to elevated cancer rates in Arroyo.

shards.title: Death from Above: Mass Drivers as Weapons of Mass Destruction
shards.summary: A think-tank white paper on the proliferation of orbital weapons platforms after the Fourth Corporate War.

shards.title: Kendachi - New Spring Catalogue
shards.summary: A weapons manufacturer's product catalogue for their spring 2077 line of monoknife variants.

shards.title: Akebono Arcade Collection
shards.summary: A digital catalogue for vintage arcade cabinet restoration parts and ROMs.

shards.title: Avian Extermination Act
shards.summary: Official legislative text of Night City's controversial act authorising mass culling of urban bird populations.

shards.title: Dubai: Sand and Death
shards.summary: A war correspondent's report from post-collapse Dubai, where Petrochem's water wars left millions dead.

shards.title: All Foods – The Future of Meat
shards.summary: A corporate puff piece on All Foods' synthetic protein programme, hiding their use of human biomass.

shards.title: CHIBA 11
shards.summary: A travel/culture piece about Chiba district in Japan, presented as an Arasaka-branded destination guide.

shards.title: Arvin Accord Redefined?
shards.summary: A policy analysis questioning whether the Arvin Accord still holds relevance in the post-war world order.

shards.title: The Unofficial Diary of Lizzy Wizzy
shards.summary: Leaked personal diary of pop icon Lizzy Wizzy. Documents her breakdown, murder of Liam Nolan, and chrome-ification.

shards.title: Ad Astra - A magazine for stellar pioneers
shards.summary: A utopian space colonisation magazine, jarringly optimistic in the context of Night City's brutality.

shards.title: Mystical Rapture in Film
shards.summary: A film criticism piece analysing spiritual and transcendence themes in cyberpunk cinema.

shards.title: Archived Conversation: Nasim Perlman and Aldo Schroder
shards.summary: Nasim and Aldo discuss the psychological effects of extended ghost-mode cyberware activation.

shards.title: Crimeblock
shards.summary: A gritty crime novel excerpt set in Heywood, following a corrupt NCPD officer.

shards.title: New United States of America Overview
shards.summary: An official NUSA government overview document outlining the political structure of the reformed United States.

shards.title: Solomon Reed – FIA Dossier
shards.summary: A classified FIA personnel dossier on Solomon Reed, deep-cover operative in Dogtown.

shards.title: Barghest Internal Communiqué
shards.summary: A Barghest internal order mentioning surveillance of a high-value NUSA asset.

shards.title: The Holy Qu'ran – SURA XVII. – The Night Journey
shards.summary: A digital copy of a Quranic surah, found in the game world — notable for its in-universe framing.

shards.title: Supernova
shards.summary: A tabloid exposé on Lizzy Wizzy's rapid rise and the suspicious death of her manager Liam Nolan.

shards.title: "Bushidō" and Neopostmodernism
shards.summary: A cultural theory essay examining how corporations like Arasaka weaponise Bushido aesthetics.

shards.title: "Liberum Arbitrium" - REVIEW
shards.summary: A literary review of a controversial novel inspired by Johnny Silverhand's life and philosophy.

shards.title: Archived Conversation: Thomas Costigan and NCPD Sgt.
shards.summary: Thomas Costigan attempts to bribe an NCPD sergeant over a dock smuggling operation.
